sections.paragraph:
ἀνάπτω fut. ἀνάψω LXX; 1 aor. ἀνῆψα LXX. Pass.: fut. ἀναφθήσομαι LXX; aor. pass. ἀνήφθην kindle (s. ἅπτω; so Eur., Hdt. et al.; PGiss 3, 8; PGM 13, 681; 2 Ch 13:11; Just.) ὕλην (cp. Polyaenus 4, 7, 9 πῦρ … ἀνῆψε τὴν ὕλην; Philo, Aet. M. 127; PsSol 12:2 πῦρ ἀνάπτον καλλονήν; Ath. 19, 1 ἀνάψας πυράν; Tat. 5, 2) a forest Js 3:5. Cp. Ac 28:2 v.l. Of fire (Eur., Or. 1137; TestAbr A 4 p. 80, 24 [Stone p. 8]=TestSol 6:10 L λύχνους; Jos., Ant. 3, 207) ἀνήφθη has been kindled=is now burning Lk 12:49 (Diod S 13, 84, 2 ὅταν ἀναφθῇ πῦρ=when a fire would be kindled, or would burn). Just., D. 8, 1 πῦρ ἐν τῇ ψυχῇ ἀνήφθη.—M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 71
